title: thaspium trifoliatum
intro: Thaspium trifoliatum, also known as meadow carrot or wild carrot, is a perennial plant that belongs to the carrot family (Apiaceae). It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antispasmodic, antiseptic, and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant has various health benefits, such as reducing pain and inflammation, relieving digestive issues, and supporting respiratory health. The herbal preparations of Thaspium trifoliatum typically involve infusions or decoctions, which can be used to make teas, tinctures, or salves for topical application.
